classification_boundary.question: On the fixed 2025 annual control surface, does continuation_release_hysteresis contribute positively, negatively, or only through compensated row changes when compared as baseline vs release_zero under the same active stack?
classification_boundary.reference_surface: fixed full-year 2025 same-stack control comparison on the landed continuation_release_hysteresis carrier, holding the active router stack constant while toggling only continuation_release_hysteresis

packet_summary.inference: On the fixed 2025 full-year control surface, the release-zero-minus-baseline top line is mixed or flat. The seam still changes many rows, but the broader annual control year cannot be reduced to a simple one-direction win from total return and final capital alone.
packet_summary.next_hypothesis: With both bad-year and positive-year annual surfaces measured, the next honest comparison is a bounded cross-year side-by-side read of where annual seam deltas stay action-neutral, versus whether one smaller row family explains any surviving annual sign difference.
